popularStory.headline: Margaret Died in Childhood
popularStory.description: Margaret (ca. 1288, Paris – after November 1294, Paris). Died in childhood, but betrothed in November 1294 (aged six) to Infante Ferdinand of Castile, later Ferdinand IV of Castile.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England於1279年9月12日出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲時，她的父親Philippe III le Hardi roi de France34歲，母親Maria van Brabant25歲。她於1299年9月9日與Edward I King of England在英國英格蘭肯特坎特布瑞 凱德拉爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1318年2月14日在英國英格蘭維爾爾馬爾波洛 聖 彼得 安德 聖 保羅享年38歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦克里斯蒂 邱奇 格也福利亞斯 紐蓋特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England于1279年9月12日出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲时，她的父亲Philippe III le Hardi roi de France34岁，母亲Maria van Brabant25岁。她于1299年9月9日与Edward I King of England在英国英格兰肯特坎特布瑞 凱德拉爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1318年2月14日在英国英格兰維爾爾馬爾波洛 聖 彼得 安德 聖 保羅享年38岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦克里斯蒂 邱奇 格也福利亞斯 紐蓋特。

nameMeanings.name: Capet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: regional variant of Chapel , from a dialect diminutive of Old French chape ‘hooded cloak, cape, hood’, or ‘hat’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of cloaks or hats, or a nickname for a habitual wearer of a distinctive cloak or hat.
Catalan: old variant or Castilianized form of   Capell   ‘hat, hood’, a cognate of 2 above.

variants.name: Capell
meanings: English: variant of Chappell ‘chapel’, from a Norman form with hard c-, applied as a topographic or occupational name, or as a habitational name for someone from any of several minor places called with this word, such as Capel in Surrey, Capel le Ferne in Kent, or Capel Saint Andrew and Capel Saint Mary in Suffolk.

English: from Middle English capel, caple, capul(le) ‘stallion, gelding; warhorse, cart horse, riding horse’, denoting someone thought to resemble a horse in some way or possibly someone whose occupation involved horses.
Catalan: from capell ‘hat, hood’, as a nickname for someone who habitually wore a hat or hood, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who made hats or hoods.

variants.name: Capeles
meanings: Hispanic (Puerto Rico): probably the plural form of Catalan   Capel  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: France
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from France (see 2 below).

French: habitational name given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris); or, in southern France, an ethnic name for someone from what is now the northern half of the country, where the langue d'oïl (Old French as distinct from Occitan) was spoken. Compare   Defrance   and   Lafrance  .
Czech (Franče): from a pet form of František, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Defrance
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Henri, Jean-Paul.

French (mainly northern): habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris). Compare France .

nameMeanings.name: Marguerite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Margaret , also used in the English-speaking world, where its use has been reinforced by the fact that the name was adopted in the 19th century for a garden flower, a large cultivated variety of daisy. Margaret was earlier used in English as a dialect word denoting the ox-eye daisy, and the French equivalent was borrowed into English just in time to catch the vogue for deriving girls' names from vocabulary words denoting flowers. See also Daisy .

en.lifeSummary: When Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England was born on 12 September 1279, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, her father, Philippe III le Hardi roi de France, was 34 and her mother, Maria van Brabant, was 25. She married Edward I King of England on 9 September 1299, in Canterbury Cathedral, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 14 February 1318, in Marlborough St Peter and St Paul, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 38, and was buried in Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England est née le 12 septembre 1279, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, son père, Philippe III le Hardi roi de France, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Maria van Brabant, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Edward I King of England le 9 septembre 1299, à Canterbury Cathedral, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 14 février 1318, à Marlborough St Peter and St Paul, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterrée à Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England am 12. September 1279 in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Philippe III le Hardi roi de France 34 und ihre Mutter Maria van Brabant 25. Sie hat am 9. September 1299 in Canterbury Cathedral, Kent, England, United Kingdom Edward I King of England geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 14. Februar 1318 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Marlborough St Peter and St Paul, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England è nata il il 12 settembre 1279 nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Philippe III le Hardi roi de France, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Maria van Brabant, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Edward I King of England il 9 settembre 1299, nel Canterbury Cathedral, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 14 febbraio 1318, nel Marlborough St Peter and St Paul, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 38 anni, e fu sepolta a Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Marguerite Capet de France Queen of Englandが1279年9月12日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスで生まれた時，父Philippe III le Hardi roi de Franceは34歳，母Maria van Brabantは25歳であった。1299年9月9日にイギリスイギリスケントカンテルブリー カスエドラルでEdward I King of Englandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1318年2月14日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアマルルボロウ スト ペター アンド スト プアウルで38歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンチリスト チャーチ グレイフリアルズ ニューガトにて埋葬された。

ko.Marlborough Castle, Marlborough, Wiltshi: 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 말를버루 스트 피터 앤드 스트 폴
ko.Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 크리스트 처르치 그리프리아르스 뉴가트
ko.lifeSummary: Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England(이)가 1279년 9월 12일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Philippe III le Hardi roi de France(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maria van Brabant(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1299년 9월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 캉르버이 캐테드럴에서 Edward I King of England(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1318년 2월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 말를버루 스트 피터 앤드 스트 폴에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 크리스트 처르치 그리프리아르스 뉴가트에 묻혔다.

pt.12 September 1279–14 February 1318: 12 de setembro de 1279–14 de fevereiro de 1318
pt.Canterbury Cathedral, Kent, England: Canterbury Cathedral, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Marlborough Castle, Marlborough, Wiltshi: Marlborough St Peter and St Paul, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London: Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England nasceu em 12 de setembro de 1279, em Paris, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Philippe III le Hardi roi de France, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Maria van Brabant, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Edward I King of England em 9 de setembro de 1299, em Canterbury Cathedral, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 14 de fevereiro de 1318, em Marlborough St Peter and St Paul, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 38 anos, e foi sepultada em Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Marlborough Castle, Marlborough, Wiltshi: марлбороую ст питир анд ст поул, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London: краист чурч грифрярс нюгэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England родилась 12 сентября 1279 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Philippe III le Hardi roi de France было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Maria van Brabant, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edward I King of England 9 сентября 1299 г. в местности кантирбури кэтедраль, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 14 февраля 1318 г. в местности марлбороую ст питир анд ст поул, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 38 лет и была похоронена в местности краист чурч грифрярс нюгэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.12 September 1279–14 February 1318: 12 de septiembre de 1279–14 de febrero de 1318
es.Canterbury Cathedral, Kent, England: Canterbury Cathedral, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.Marlborough Castle, Marlborough, Wiltshi: Marlborough St Peter and St Paul, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London: Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England nació el 12 de septiembre de 1279, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Philippe III le Hardi roi de France, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Maria van Brabant, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Edward I King of England el 9 de septiembre de 1299, en Canterbury Cathedral, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 14 de febrero de 1318, en Marlborough St Peter and St Paul, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultada en Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
